ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-03-2020 Continued To Mar-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Heather Ledgerwood for Executor, Elizabeth Dahlhoff.

The Court announced the tentative ruling. Ms. Ledgerwood stated that she is contesting the continuance, that she received no notice from the Probate Examiner two weeks prior to be put on alert to check for an early tentative, that this continuance will be a hardship on the estate, citing IRC 663B: 65-day rule expires on March 5th where the estate would be subject to 50% of income as oppose to 30%. The Court noted since it does not have the papers and has not reviewed the papers it does not know the alternative but to continue the matter, and further noted that there were prior opportunities for counsel to resolve this problem. Ms. Ledgerwood stated that she checked the tentative ruling on Friday, that she was expecting a pre-grant, was surprised to see the continuance, and that she inquired if she could file a preliminary distribution by ex parte. The Court noted that preliminary distributions are not heard by ex parte. Ms. Ledgerwood stated this is an exigent circumstance due to taxes, that this is a small estate with a surviving spouse, that it is community property, that waivers were filed, and the estate is going to be harmed. Ms. Ledgerwood further stated that papers are visible online, and inquired about effecting change to the local rules. The Court noted there is a local rule process available on the court's website and counsel is welcome to pursue them.

The court adopts the tentative ruling to continue the accounting petition re: fees and final distribution to March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-21-2019 Granted Per Written Order
